issues.title: 2.5.1 breaks /dev/dri detection - Hardware acceleration is dead
issues.next_action_reason: Confirmed 2.5.1 regression: /dev/dri/card0 no longer created; community workaround is `modprobe vgem` (+ boot it) plus VA-API env vars. Acknowledge, label workaround-available, route to GPU/graphics.
issues.suggested_reply: Thanks for the detailed reports and screenshots. Confirming the regression: since 2.5.1 `/dev/dri/card0` is no longer created (only `renderD128`), which breaks device detection for apps that open card0. As a workaround, loading `vgem` (`sudo modprobe vgem`, and add it to load on boot) restores a card node, and D3D12 Mesa VA-API works via `renderD128`. We're routing this to the graphics team — thanks for your patience.

issues.title: WSLg 1.0.71: GUI session freezes, then Weston becomes unkillable in /mnt/shared_memory virtiofs
issues.next_action_reason: New, well-documented severe freeze on latest 1.0.71. We owe a first acknowledgement + ask for a couple of targeted artifacts (weston.log, dmesg around the hang) to progress it.
issues.suggested_reply: Thanks for the exceptionally detailed report, @Mygod — the isolation work (confirming the JVMs aren't deadlocked via `xset`/`jcmd`, and that Weston becomes unkillable in the `/mnt/shared_memory` virtiofs path) is very helpful. To let us dig into the compositor side, could you attach: (1) `/mnt/wslg/weston.log` from a session that hit the freeze, (2) `dmesg` from inside the WSL2 VM around the time of the hang (in case the unkillable state is a virtiofs/D-state issue), and (3) whether it still reproduces with `autoMemoryReclaim` disabled? That will help us tell whether this is the compositor stalling or the shared-memory mount wedging the process.
